Pakistan Rushes to Secure LNG as Summer Power Demand Surges
headlines.summary: Pakistan has issued a new tender for LNG, to the amount of 1 million tons, The Nation reported today, citing the seasonal increase in electricity demand as summer heat sets in. headlines.title: Oil Prices Dip as Israel-Lebanon Ceasefire Revives Iran Deal Hopes
headlines.summary: Oil prices were under pressure in early Asian trade on Thursday following the announcement of a ceasefire agreement between Israel and Lebanon. headlines.title: Europe Scrambles to Contain the Energy Shock
headlines.summary: The European Union and the Eurozone are feeling the energy shock as the Iran war enters its fourth month. The spike in oil and gas prices amid the Middle East crisis is raising inflation and moderating economic growth expectations in the EU and the Euro area, which are grappling

headlines.title: Energy Costs Are Catching Up With Big Tech
headlines.summary: Artificial intelligence is all the rage these days, and so is its energy consumption. headlines.title: A Barrel Trapped Behind Hormuz Isn't Spare Capacity
headlines.summary: At the start of 2026, oil traders were preparing for a glut. Supply growth was expected to outpace demand growth. OPEC+ was gradually returning barrels to the market. headlines.title: Kuwait Says Oil Output Won't Recover for 10-12 Weeks After Hormuz Reopens
headlines.summary: Kuwait Petroleum Company expects it will take considerably longer to restore oil production than many traders appear to assume if the Strait of Hormuz reopens in the coming days.
